tool_args.label: Capital Program Committee Meeting Book - September 2024

payload.title: Capital Program Committee Meeting
payload.description: Capital Program Committee Meeting book containing meeting agenda, minutes from July 29 2024, 2024-2025 work plan, safety report, Second Quarter 2024 Traffic Light Report, capital program commitments and completions, funding status, and procurement actions.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Responsible for capital program delivery including Penn Station Access, Second Avenue Subway Phase 2, and Interborough Express.

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

payload.description: Capital Program Committee Meeting held from 1:00 - 2:00 PM ET to review capital program status, Second Quarter 2024 Traffic Light Report, safety report, commitments and completions, and procurement actions.

Will deliver four new accessible stations to the East Bronx, reduce commute times to Midtown by up to 50 minutes, and bring train service to within a mile of 500,000 additional residents. Early challenges with Amtrak caused delays, but new coordination strategies are helping. Next steps include completing schedule re-sequencing and creating cost and schedule efficiencies.

Will service 100,000 riders per day and reduce commute times by up to 20 minutes. Substantial completion and budget remain on track. Lessons learned from Phase 1 saved $1.3 billion. Team will secure access to utility agreements and easements.

Transformative light rail train project in existing rail corridor between Bay Ridge and Jackson Heights. C&D is pursuing FTA funding, engaging public outreach including 35+ meetings. Design progressing with survey and design work prioritizing minimizing bridge reconstruction, improving subway connections, reducing station costs. Next steps include continuing outreach and preparing RFP for preliminary engineering.

payload.raw_value_text: 275 projects were reviewed in the Traffic Light Report (TLR) for Cost and Schedule adherence
13 projects, 12 in construction and 1 in design, triggered a variance
reduce commute times to Midtown by up to 50 minutes

issues: metric_claim payload with raw_value_text should include normalized numeric value or value_min/value_max

reduce commute times to Midtown by up to 50 minutes

payload.raw_value_text: lessons learned from Phase 1... have already saved the project $1.3 billion in costs
900,000 Brooklyn and Queens residents who live within 0.5 miles of the proposed route

tool_args.label: Penn Station Access Commute Time Benefit

payload.claim_text: Penn Station Access will deliver four new accessible stations to the East Bronx, reduce commute times to Midtown by up to 50 minutes, and bring train service to within a mile of 500,000 additional residents
payload.subject: Penn Station Access

tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 Ridership and Commute Benefit

payload.claim_text: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 will service 100,000 riders per day and reduce commute times by up to 20 minutes. The project's substantial completion and budget remain on track while the team is working to identify cost contingencies and integrate contractor input early on.
payload.subject: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2

tool_args.label: SAS Phase 2 Cost Savings from Phase 1 Lessons

payload.claim_text: The team continues to implement lessons learned from Phase 1, such as reusing old tunnels and reducing station size and back of house resources, which have already saved the project $1.3 billion in costs.
payload.subject: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2

payload.description: Potential expansion project mentioned as advancing planning in the 2025-2029 Capital Plan discussion.

tool_args.label: Staten Island North Shore Busway Planning

payload.description: Potential expansion project mentioned as advancing planning in the 2025-2029 Capital Plan.
Potential expansion project mentioned as advancing planning in the 2025-2029 Capital Plan.

Meeting called to order at 9:19 AM, included public comments period (10 speakers), President's Report on 2025-2029 Capital Plan, procurement action, and adjournment. Reconvened at 3:38 PM for Integrated Projects Reports (Penn Station Access, Second Avenue Subway Phase 2, Interborough Express).

payload.claim_text: The 2025-2029 Capital Plan is due by statute on October 1, and the Board must vote in September. The plan is being developed using both a bottom-up and top-down approach. It will include three categories of investment: steady investment adjusted for inflation, increased investment, and expansion opportunities. The plan does not include projects paused due to Congestion Pricing.
payload.subject: 2025-2029 Capital Plan

payload.claim_text: The new Capital Plan calls for an investment in 2000 railcars for the subways and railroads, tripling the investment of the previous plan. This will mean modern cars and increased reliability for riders on the 1, 2, 6, B, D, J, N, Q, and W lines.
payload.subject: 2025-2029 Capital Plan

payload.raw_value_text: 66 safety incidents were reported in July 2024 (26 incidents) and August 2024 (40 incidents)
53 Lost Time incidents have been reported YTD (through August 31, 2024)
40 Recordable incidents have been reported YTD (through August 31, 2024)

payload.description: Capital Plan due October 1, 2024. Developed using bottom-up and top-down approach. Includes investment in 2000 railcars (tripling previous plan), power upgrades, zero emissions buses and charging infrastructure, signal modernization, accessibility expansion, and expansion opportunities including Interborough Express, 125 St Tunnel, Staten Island North Shore Busway, and Ridgewood Busway.

payload.claim_text: Modification No. 6 to Contract GFM-526 with Restani Construction Corp for $7,964,000 to increase certain repair quantities and extend term by seven months for as-needed roadway repairs for all MTA Bridges and Tunnels bridges and roadways. Contract extended from December 31, 2024 to July 31, 2025.
payload.subject: Contract GFM-526

payload.claim_text: Ratification of Modification No. 01 to Contract MN146606 with Urban EcoSpaces, Inc. for $113,520 to furnish and install additional roofing components for Node House roof replacement at 23 Metro-North Railroad Communications Node Houses.
payload.subject: Contract MN146606

payload.claim_text: Ratification of Modification No. 46 to Contract VM014 with Schindler Elevator Corporation for $1,569,457 to extend maintenance of elevators and escalators in Grand Central Madison for up to seven weeks (July 29 to September 15, 2024) while transitioning to a long-term maintenance contract.
payload.subject: Contract VM014

tool_args.label: 2025-2029 Capital Plan presented at July 2024 CPC Meeting

payload.description: 2025-2029 Capital Plan update was presented at the July 29, 2024 CPC Meeting by C&D President Torres-Springer.
C&D Project CEO Jolyon Handler presented the Penn Station Access project update.
Saqib Rizwan, C&D Project CEO for Second Avenue Subway Phase 2, presented the update.
Capital Plan will include funding for the Interborough Express.
Plan will allow for advancing planning on 125 St Tunnel, Staten Island North Shore Busway, and Ridgewood Busway.
